name: Chorizo (Mexican Sausage) #1
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Dry sherry (brandy may be substituted)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the pork meat and fat thoroughly.
2. Add paprika, chili powder, black pepper, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, ginger, oregano, cumin, garlic, salt, vinegar, and sherry to the mixture. Mix well with hands until everything is evenly distributed.
3. Allow the mixture to rest in a covered container at cool temperature for 24 hours, or better yet, for 2-3 days.
4. Form the mixture into patties and sauté.
5. Alternatively, you can force the mixture into sausage casings and hang them in a cool place to dry. This is best done during cold weather and in a breezy location to aid in drying.
